questions.question: In the following questions, four words are given, out of which only one word is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spellings of the other words are: engagement, demonstrable and pernicious. B is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, four words are given, out of which only one word is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spellings of the other words are: abysmal, provocative and poignant. B is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. Our hosts were thanked by us for their kindness and hospitality.
options: We thank our hosts for their kindness and hospitality.
We thanked our hosts for their kindness and hospitality.
We will thank our hosts for their kindness and hospitality.
We are thanking our hosts for their kindness and hospitality.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in passive voice and 'were thanked' is in the simple past tense. A is incorrect as 'thank' is in simple present tense. C is incorrect as 'will thank' is in simple future tense. D is incorrect as 'are thanking' is in present continuous tense. B is the right answer, as 'thanked' is in simple past tense.

questions.question: In the below question, sentence/passage is split into four parts and named P, Q, R & S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find out which of the four combinations is correct. P. She was alone till evening, when the same woman brought her tea on a tray with fresh cakes and tried to talk her into giving the maid's job a trial. Q. The woman said that she would eat it when she was hungry enough and went away, leaving the porridge behind. R. The girl refused it and demanded her clothes and her release. S. Presently the younger woman arrived with a bowl of porridge.
questions.explanation: Sentences S and R are a pair: S tells us about a bowl of porridge that was brought by a woman, while R builds up on it, and tells us that the person for whom this had been brought refused to eat it. Options A and D are eliminated. It is clear (following chronological order) that Sentence Q should follow the pair SR. Option C is eliminated. Option B is the right answer.

questions.question: In the below question, sentence/passage is split into four parts and named P, Q, R & S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find out which of the four combinations is correct. P. He was tired and beginning to be out of temper with Kevin. Q. He got up to help himself to more whisky, holding out his other hand for Robert's glass. R. He shook his head without lifting his gaze from the fire. S. But Robert was in no mood for conviviality.
questions.explanation: Sentences Q and S are a pair: Q tells us about Kevin's friendly gesture towards Robert, while S builds up on it, and tells us how this gesture was received. Options A and D are eliminated. It is clear that sentence R should follow the pair QS, since it tells us about his further response, and follows the chronological order of events. Option C is eliminated. Option B is the right answer.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the Idiom/Phrase underlined in the sentence. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. I agreed to the new proposal at the drop of a hat
options: with a lot of queries in mind
questions.explanation: "At the drop of a hat" means without any hesitation/instantly.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the Idiom/Phrase underlined in the sentence. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase and mark it. To ace this quiz competition, we must keep our fingers on the pulse as regards technology.
options: Put up a plan or idea into action
Be constantly aware of the most recent developments
Be serious about what you announce
questions.explanation: 'Keep your fingers on the pulse' refers to being constantly aware of the recent developments.

questions.question: According to the passage, Vermont strain of Merino sheep
options: was first to be discovered by historians to be extinct
had gone extinct by the time historians searched it
was displayed in Old Sturbridge Village
was first one to become extinct
questions.explanation: Solution: Refer to the third line. It says enthusiasts discovered that sheep had gone extinct. The passage does not say anything about other animals.

questions.question: The organization that conducted the first survey on American livestock breeds was
questions.explanation: Solution: The passage does not mention when or who conducted first survey. Only that organisations conducted their first surveys.

questions.question: American livestock breed enthusiasts
options: were not at all concerned with disappearance of many traditional livestock breeds.
Comprised only scientists, farmers, and historians
were not aware of disappearance of many traditional livestock breeds during 60s and 70s
were aware of the disappearance of many traditional livestock breeds during 60s and 70s
questions.explanation: Solution: Refer to the second line. It says that they became increasingly aware of the disappearance.

questions.question: Rare Breeds Canada
options: Changed its name more than twice
Has been in collaboration with American Minor Breeds Conservancy
Has worked only on the breeds in Canada
questions.explanation: Solution: Towards the end of the passage it says it collaborates with American Minor Breeds Conservancy. Rest are not mentioned.

questions.question: American Minor Breeds Conservancy
options: was incorporated in Vermont, Old Sturbridge Village
It was the first United States organization focused on preserving common breeds of livestock
remains the preeminent organization in this field in the United States.
Is in collaboration with Rare Breeds Survival Trust
questions.explanation: Solution: The answer is clearly given in the 6th line of the passage. Hence, c is the correct option.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences and mark it. One who cannot refrain from the urge of stealing items
questions.explanation: Larcenist and Purloiner mean someone who steals. A burglar is the one who illegally enters a building with intent to commit a crime, especially theft. One who cannot refrain from the urge of stealing items/things is called a Kleptomaniac.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks The guilt of these homicides lies upon every man and woman who, intentionally or by cold _________, helps to keep up social conditions that drive human beings to despair.
questions.explanation: Option A has the word 'apathy' meaning lack of enthusiasm or care. Although it fits somewhat in the sentence, it is too extreme for the context. Option C is eliminated, as it has the word 'nonchalance' meaning calm and relaxed, which is not exactly the meaning which the sentence seeks to convey. Option D is eliminated, as it has the word 'aloofness' which means not friendly or sociable. This is slightly different from what the sentence seeks to convey. Option B is the right answer, as 'indifference' which means a state of being unconcerned, most suitably fits the context of the sentence.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences. A person who changes sides.
questions.explanation: Teetotaller: One who never takes strong drinks. Topsy-Turvy: Upside down. Diplomatic: concerning the art of dealing with people in a tactful way.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. ODIOUS
questions.explanation: Odious means hateful, disgusting, offensive etc. Example: Even the smell of meat is odious to some people.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. CASTIGATE
questions.explanation: Castigate means to criticise severely; to chastise, rebuke, scold, etc. Example: Jayesh's Boss chastised him for leaving the office early without finishing his work. Encage means to confine or put inside a cage.

questions.question: Choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Alleviate
questions.explanation: "Alleviate" means "to make the problem more bearable or less severe". The word opposite in meaning is "Aggravate" which means "to make a problem more serious or severe"

questions.question: Select the word which is opposite in meaning to the word in capitals. ASPERSION
questions.explanation: Aspersion means an attack on the reputation or integrity of someone or something. Hence, the word opposite in meaning is "Appreciation". Vilification- to say or write unpleasant things about someone or something. Denouncement- to criticize someone or someone strongly and publicly.

questions.question: Change the form from Direct to Indirect Speech or vice-versa The father said, "Bravo!, brilliantly done, son."
options: The father exclaimed with joy saying that his son did extremely well.
The father applauded his son saying that he had done extremely well.
The father exclaimed with joy that his son had done extremely well.
The father shouted with joy that his son was doing extremely well.
questions.explanation: Present perfect in direct speech changes to past perfect in indirect speech which is reflected in option b. Option c comes close but without verb "saying", it fails to express the intended meaning, making option c the correct option.

questions.question: Sentences given with blanks to be filled in with an appropriate word(s). Four alternatives are suggested for each question. Choose the correct alternative out of the four. That Shakespeare was one of the most talented writers ___ an understatement to those who are really familiar with the field of literature.
questions.explanation: The subject in this sentence is not 'writers' but the entire statement "Shakespeare was one of the most talented writers", and hence the subject is singular. Also, the statement is a current one and not from past. Hence, the tense will be present tense. So, the correct word to be used is "is".

questions.question: A sentence or a part of the sentence is underlined. Below are given alternatives to the underlined part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is needed, choose "No Improvement". The truck dumped a larger number of garbage in the area than is average for a month.
questions.explanation: In the sentence, there is a comparison between two things: average amount of garbage and the present amount of garbage. Thus, a comparative degree is required. Also, 'number' refers to individual things that can be counted (e.g. pens) and 'amount' refers to something that cannot be counted (e.g. water).

questions.question: In the following questions, the parts of sentence is given in the options. Some part may contain grammatical error. If any part contains error then mark that option as the answer and if there is no error then mark "no error" as the answer. Kinds who have had a good pre-school education(A)/ are most likely to outdo other childrens at(B)/ school even if they belong to rich parents(C)/ No error (D).
questions.explanation: 'Children' is itself plural. Singular noun is 'Child'. Hence the error is in B part. 'Childrens' is a wrong word.

questions.question: Some of the sentences are grammatically correct and some are incorrect. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and mark that part. If there is no error mark that part as your answer. Concurrent with this programme (A)/ educational institutions may be urged (B)/ to inculcate patriotism in each and every one of its pupils (C)/ No error (D)
questions.explanation: Here the subject is institutions, so replace 'its' by 'their'.

questions.question: A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given for the underlined part which will improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and mark your answer accordingly. In case no improvement is needed, "No improvement" will be your answer. Despite not getting approval from the leader, we decided to carry out the plan.
questions.explanation: The correct phrasal verb to be used here is 'carry out', as it means to execute a plan. 'Carry on' means to continue. 'Carry off' means to physically carry something away. 'Carry by' is grammatically incorrect. D is the right answer.
